title: Fix handling of socket connection refusal.
body:
Currently if the socket was never started you get an error about
the service being started.  But if the service was started and later
stopped, you get a useless error.

This change causes the error to always be the same for connection refused.

The error message was also repeating the address twice which looked bad.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
@jwhonce @baude PTAL
BTW pypodman does not work in release because we did not install python3-psutil as a requires in Fedora or RHEL spec.

LGTM

@rhatdan I'll put together a PR for the spec file update. https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1290

:pushpin: Commit 858f303 has been approved by `baude`

@jwhonce The reason the second field was removed, was that it was printing twice.  The first field was enough to get it printed.
